title: eryngium palmeri
intro: Eryngium palmeri, also known as Palmer's Eryngo, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its tall spikes of blue or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Eryngium palmeri include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Eryngium palmeri can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
